summary: Summary: Chronologically, the conversation opens with Speaker2 (Amanda) accusing Speaker1 angrily and with frustration of tormenting her over Peter Burden; Speaker1 responds neutral, dismissing the claims as jealousy, then briefly becomes happy recalling a brooch memory, which only deepens Speaker2’s frustration. As they move to Amanda and Peter’s divorce, Speaker1 shifts to neutral and frustration and downplays the discussion, while Speaker2 moves from neutral to anger over brandy consumption and uses sarcasm that escalates the exchange. When Peter’s past behavior is raised, Speaker2 reacts with anger and frustration at Speaker1’s remarks; Speaker1 retaliates angrily with insinuations about intoxication, prompting Speaker2 to deny it and grow more frustrated and angry. The argument intensifies: Speaker1 becomes increasingly frustrated and angry, rebukes the other as nosy and boorish, hurls insults (including “I hate you”) and storms out; Speaker2 alternates frustration and anger, shouts at Peter to turn off a radio and delivers a vicious personal attack, leaving the encounter ended on a dramatic, angry note.
